_help: This is your project's local copy of the BDD manifest. You can define custom UI steps here for the AI to use. For the latest default manifest, see the markdown-bdd-transpiler repository.

available_steps.description: Navigates the browser page to a relative URL path.
Fills out a text input area (e.g. textbox, textarea) identified by its ARIA role and accessible name.
Handles universal interactions (e.g., clicking, toggling, checking) on an element identified by its ARIA role (e.g., 'button', 'link', 'checkbox') and accessible name.
Clicks an element identified solely by its visible text. Use this when the element lacks a clear ARIA role.
Clicks an element identified by its exact visible text. Use this to avoid Playwright strict-mode violations when multiple similar text strings exist.
Clicks an element identified by its data-testid attribute. Use this as a structural fallback for legacy applications.
Fills out a text input area identified by its data-testid attribute.
Asserts the state (e.g., 'visible', 'hidden', 'enabled', 'disabled') of an element identified by its ARIA role and accessible name.
Asserts the state (e.g., 'visible', 'hidden', 'enabled', 'disabled') of an element identified solely by its visible text.
Asserts the state (e.g., 'visible', 'hidden', 'enabled', 'disabled') of an element identified by its exact visible text. Use this to avoid Playwright strict-mode violations.
Asserts the state (e.g., 'visible', 'hidden', 'enabled', 'disabled') of an element identified by its data-testid attribute.
